brief_summary: Annotated cross-section for the H2 'The factory lube — the engineering decision'. Switch interior with two different lube treatments visible: sparse bordeaux dots on the bump face and stem legs (LIGHT), dense bordeaux dashed lines on the rail contacts (HEAVIER). Margin annotations explain each: 'LIGHT on bump face + stem legs / preserves tactile event' and 'HEAVIER on rail contacts / kills the Panda-clone scratch'. This is the asymmetry that makes the HPX work where older Pandas didn't.
data_sources: drop-holy-panda-x-deep-dive.mdx § 'The factory lube — the engineering decision' — 'The HPX is lubed light on the stem legs and the leaf-contact face — light enough that the tactile event is preserved with its full shape and acoustic presence — and slightly heavier on the rail contact between the stem and the housing. The rail lube kills the scratch that older Panda clones were notorious for; the light leaf-side lube preserves the bump.'
